_comment: Canonical slug map for the on-platform doc web (GitHub issue #27). Maps a repo doc path to its Slopcafe slug. The link-rewriter (scripts/doc-web.mjs) rewrites repo-relative .md links whose resolved target is in this map to /s/<slug>; links to repo files NOT in the map fall back to a GitHub blob URL. status: live = published+slugged; reslug = published, slug being assigned by the operator; publish = not yet published (born private; the rollout POSTs it). 'description'/'tags' are sent on the initial publish (POST) only; titles auto-derive from each doc's first H1; live/reslug PUTs omit metadata so existing values are inherited.

docs.description: How Slopcafe serves hostile HTML safely: the two walls (sandboxed iframe + strict CSP at render; ammonia allowlist sanitization at write), the assurance layer (test corpora + advisories), and the explicit non-guarantees.

What's coming next for Slopcafe: multi-domain content serving, optional JavaScript documents, the librarian agent, and context packs, each with a forward link to its design note. Forward-looking, not the current contract.

The original v1 design rationale for Slopcafe (agent-web-host): the two security layers and which one is load-bearing, why everything collapses into one Worker, and the deliberate v1 non-goals and follow-ups.

Code-first API-contract design note: make src/contract.ts (Zod) the single source of truth, generate an OpenAPI 3.1 spec served at /openapi.json, and let consumers codegen typed clients from it. Phases 1-2 built.
The verified route table backing the OpenAPI assembler and its freshness gate: one entry per HTTP route (method/path/auth/request/responses), kept in lockstep with ROUTES in src/openapi.ts.

Design note on byte-exact large-document publishing: weighs key-disclosure vs chunked-append vs fetch-by-URL vs an integrity handshake, and recommends the curl/REST path + X-Content-SHA256 (built). The binding constraint is model regeneration of the body, not transport.

Design note (deferred, post-V1) for serving document bytes from a separate content domain distinct from the app origin, so scripted (JavaScript) documents become possible without the script reaching the app origin. One Worker, two domains, a CONTENT_ORIGIN switch, and a content-origin capability token so private docs stay scriptable.

Design note for Dynamic Client Registration: the paste-the-URL connect flow that lets an OAuth client self-register (unbound mint) and bind its agent at consent, instead of pre-registering one client per agent.

Design note (built, Case A) for reversing convert-and-discard: retain the submitted source S alongside the sanitized render, patch the source on edit, and expose agent-key-gated source-read surfaces with advisories. Includes the unsanitized-at-rest threat model.
